title: Consiliency authority event protocol
$comment: One single-sourced, cryptographically-authenticated authority event. SEPARATION OF POWERS: `core` is the Portal-signed, ledger-slot-INDEPENDENT payload the human decision attests; the Ed25519 signature covers EXACTLY the canonical bytes of `core` (see docs/design/authority-event-canonical-bytes.md) and nothing else. `chain` is ledger-appended metadata OUTSIDE the signature, so spec can append it without re-signing and thus never becomes an authority minter. Verification uses the PINNED authority-key-registry, keyed by core.key_id — never the event's self-declared signature fields.

core.$comment: SIGNED. These are the only fields the Ed25519 signature covers. Chain-position-independent so Portal can sign before spec appends to the ledger.

decision_id.$comment: The Portal-minted DECISION UUID = the authority identity (amendment #1). NOT the phaseId; phase/subgraph are scoped audience fields below.

audience.$comment: Scoped audience the decision applies to. phase/subgraph are SCOPED fields, not the identity (amendment #1).

cert_digest.$comment: MANDATORY (N4). Binds the event to the SPECIFIC cert the human saw — not merely its canon_version.

approver.$comment: The approver identity. Verification requires the signing key (core.key_id) to be registered to this approver (amendment #4).

key_id.$comment: Identity of the signing key, IN the signed core so the signature commits to which key signed. Verification selects the pubkey+scheme from the pinned registry by this value.

phase_loop_driver_allowed.$comment: MUST be false — authority is minted in the Portal, never by the phase-loop driver/leaf.

chain.$comment: UNSIGNED, ledger-appended (spec). Appending or mutating this block does NOT invalidate the core signature — that independence is a conformance vector.

signature.$comment: The signature bytes plus self-declared scheme/key_id. Verification IGNORES scheme/key_id here in favor of core.key_id -> the pinned registry; these echoes exist only for transport legibility. A scheme that disagrees with the registry is an algorithm-confusion REJECT.

metadataSafeString.$comment: Printable ASCII 0x21..0x7E excluding double-quote and backslash. The AUDIENCE_VALUE_RE metadata-safe set that makes the canonical bytes cross-language identical.

timestamp.$comment: RFC3339 UTC, fixed width, 'Z' only — sorts lexicographically == chronologically.
